idea: By mid-2025, at least one major U.S. city (population >500k) will pass a law banning the use of AI-based hiring tools for automated resume screening or interviews.
In horticulture, germination test seeds (often treated with fungicides) can be planted in a perimeter around crops to create a natural pest deterrent, as the coatings repel common soil pests.
Removing a major urban highway can reduce overall travel time for commuters, even without alternative road expansions.
The default random seed '12345' used in many programming environments creates hidden correlations that can systematically bias simulation results in scientific studies.

By October 2025, the US Department of Energy will announce a breakthrough in AI-accelerated fusion simulations that reduces simulation time from weeks to hours, but will also reveal that the AI model contains a critical flaw making its predictions unreliable.

Create a service that rents out 'silence'—a physical, soundproofed, windowless room with no electronics, for 30-minute intervals, in high-traffic urban areas, targeting people who need to make difficult phone calls or have private conversations.
By June 2025, Anthropic will temporarily disable Claude's code-generation feature due to a discovered vulnerability where the model outputs semantically correct but cryptographically weak keys.
